summary: Updated the repository's dtk-template-derived build tooling in configure.py and tools/project.py. The PR added optional Ninja binary selection, changed warning CLI choices, introduced extab post-processing build rules, added path-case checking for source/asm paths, adjusted objdiff.json generation and dependencies, worked around a dtk-template assembler depfile issue, and removed the old host-compiler source build path. The decomp.dev GALE01 report said there were no binary changes.

key_files.changes: Added --ninja BINARY option, stored it as config.ninja_path, and changed --warn choices to all/off/error with explicit dest/type.

Updated ProjectConfig/Object build generation: extab_padding option, Ninja path propagation to objdiff custom_make, path-case checks, MWCC extab rules, GNU as dependency workaround, objdiff.json output/dependency wiring, and removal of host build support.

what_changed: Added parser argument --ninja with type Path and optional help text, then assigned args.ninja to config.ninja_path.
Changed configure.py --warn choices from off/on/all to all/off/error while keeping default off.
Added Object option extab_padding and defaulted it to None during object resolution.
Removed host-related object state and host build generation: host option default, host_obj_path, host_source_inputs, host_cc/host_cpp rules, and all_source_host phony target were deleted.
Added ProjectConfig.ninja_path so generated objdiff configuration can use an absolute custom Ninja binary instead of always using literal ninja.

Added check_path_case(path), backed by a cached os.listdir lookup, to warn when the requested path casing differs from the actual filesystem entry and to exit if a path cannot be resolved or accessed.
Applied check_path_case to existing source paths and assembly override paths before generating C/C++ or asm build rules.
Added MWCC extab post-processing rules mwcc_extab and mwcc_sjis_extab using dtk extab clean --padding "$extab_padding" $out $out.
Extended non-Windows transform_dep handling to the new extab MWCC command variants and implicit dependency lists.
Changed C/C++ build-rule selection from a simple shift_jis ternary to selecting among mwcc, mwcc_sjis, mwcc_extab, and mwcc_sjis_extab based on shift_jis and extab_padding.
Encoded extab_padding into a lowercase two-digit hex string via ''.join(f'{i:02x}' for i in obj.options['extab_padding']).
Modified the GNU as rule to stop emitting/using a depfile and instead add build/include/macros.inc as an implicit dependency, citing encounter/dtk-template issue 51.
Changed assembly override selection so asm_path is only used when not already linking a built source object.
Made report generation implicitly depend on objdiff.json as well as objdiff and all_source.
Made the configure Ninja rule output both build.ninja and objdiff.json.
smart_moves: Kept the dtk-template update non-invasive to game code; the automated GALE01 report showed no changes.
Handled extab cleanup as dedicated Ninja rule variants rather than embedding conditional shell logic at every build site.
Preserved Shift JIS support while adding extab post-processing by creating a separate mwcc_sjis_extab path.
Cached directory listings in check_path_case, avoiding repeated os.listdir calls while still catching case mismatches that can break cross-platform builds.
Threaded custom Ninja selection into objdiff's custom_make field, which helps users whose Ninja binary is not named or located as plain ninja.
Explicitly wired objdiff.json into configure outputs and report dependencies, making generated config changes visible to Ninja's dependency graph.
Documented the GNU as depfile change with a direct dtk-template issue link, making the workaround traceable.
decomp_lessons: Build-tool template updates can affect reproducibility even when no decompiled C code changes; always check the project report for binary deltas.
When adding object-level build behavior, prefer an option like extab_padding that defaults to None and only changes rule selection when explicitly configured.
For cross-platform decomp repositories, path-case validation is valuable because macOS/Windows may hide casing mistakes that fail on case-sensitive filesystems.
Generated files such as objdiff.json should be declared as Ninja outputs/dependencies when downstream report targets consume them.
If external template tooling has a known depfile bug, encode the workaround in the build graph with a comment and an issue link rather than silently relying on stale dependencies.
Removing unused host-compiler helper targets simplifies build graphs, but future consumers should confirm there is no remaining workflow depending on all_source_host.
naming_conventions: ProjectConfig fields use snake_case path names such as sjiswrap_path, objdiff_path, and the newly added ninja_path.
Object build options use snake_case keys such as extab_padding, shift_jis, mw_version, and progress_category.
Ninja command and implicit-dependency variables follow paired names like mwcc_cmd/mwcc_implicit, mwcc_extab_cmd/mwcc_extab_implicit, and mwcc_sjis_extab_cmd/mwcc_sjis_extab_implicit.
Ninja rule names describe the compiler path and feature combination: mwcc, mwcc_sjis, mwcc_extab, mwcc_sjis_extab.
Generated phony/report targets retain descriptive names such as all_source and report; the removed host aggregate was all_source_host.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added dtk extab clean post-processing after MWCC for objects with extab_padding, allowing exception table padding cleanup to be represented as a build rule.
For extab padding, the configured byte list is converted to a concatenated hex string before being passed to dtk extab clean --padding.
Disabled GNU as depfile/deps emission in the Ninja rule and added macros.inc directly as an implicit dependency as a workaround for encounter/dtk-template issue 51.
Ran dtk elf fixup after GNU as output generation, preserving the existing assembler output normalization step.
Only falls back to assembly overrides when the source object is not being linked, reducing unintended replacement of successfully built source objects.

review_feedback.feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find object configs using extab_padding after PR 1829
Search for remaining references to host or all_source_host after host build removal
Check whether configure.py --warn error is consumed consistently by tools/project.py
Audit path casing warnings from check_path_case on case-sensitive filesystems
Review dtk-template issue 51 status to see whether the macros.inc implicit dependency workaround can be removed
Find PRs that modify objdiff.json generation or custom_make after adding ninja_path
